name: South Korea AI Basic Act (Framework Act on AI Industry Promotion)
summary: South Korea's AI Basic Act is one of the first comprehensive national AI laws globally. It establishes a regulatory framework promoting AI industry development while managing risks from high-impact AI systems. The law creates the AI Committee under the Prime Minister, mandates impact assessments for high-impact AI, and sets transparency and reliability requirements.
keyRequirements: AI Committee established under the Prime Minister for national AI policy coordination
High-impact AI designation for systems affecting life, safety, and fundamental rights
AI impact assessments required for high-impact AI systems before deployment
Transparency obligations including disclosure of AI-generated content and AI interaction
AI reliability standards and certification framework
Support measures for AI industry development, research, and talent cultivation
User protection measures including right to explanation of AI-driven decisions
penalties: Fines up to KRW 30 million for failure to comply with high-impact AI obligations. Administrative corrective orders and improvement directives. The AI Committee coordinates enforcement across sector-specific regulators.
